15.reason: [The condition checks only one side of the range for type validity; e.g., it checks s[j-1] >= '0' but not s[j-1] <= '9', and similarly for letters. This allows invalid cross-type cases like 'a'-'9' to pass if s[j-1] < s[j+1]. Also, accessing s[j-1] when j=0 or s[j+1] when j=i leads to out-of-bounds reads.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition checks only one side of the range for type validity; e.g., it checks s[j-1] >= '0' but not s[j-1] <= '9', and similarly for letters. This allows invalid cross-type cases like 'a'-'9' to pass if s[j-1] < s[j+1]. Also, accessing s[j-1] when j=0 or s[j+1] when j=i leads to out-of-bounds reads.]
